mission.title: connect-rpc: More request information in Interceptor.WrapStreamContext()
mission.description: More request information in Interceptor.WrapStreamContext(). This issue affects 1+ users.

steps.title: Identify connect-rpc feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your connect-rpc deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=connect-rpc
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=connect-rpc -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: More request information in Interceptor.WrapStreamContext()

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant connect-rpc resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```

steps.title: Confirm More request information in Interceptor.WrapStream is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=connect-rpc -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Streaming interceptors aren't very useful in their current state. Rather
than wrapping user code, they wrap the the networking primitives used by
the user's code. This is a subtle distinction, but it makes it
impossible to write a panic-recovering interceptor. It also makes common
server-side interceptors (e.g., authentication) needlessly difficult to
write.

This commit reworks the Sender and Rec. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with connect-rpc installed or the issue environment reproducible.
